articles.title: A goal we'll be talking about for decades
articles.description: Like everyone else I'm of course talking about Daizen Maeda's wonder goal from Sunday's 3-1 win over Rangers, a strike I've now watched literally dozens of times, and from every conceivable angle. Maeda's game has always been about chaos, energy, intensity and relentless running. It's been about closing defenders down, making impossible recoveries and dragging Celtic forward through sheer force of will.

articles.title: Popp hoping to win one last women's German Cup with Wolfsburg
articles.description: German football icon Alexandra Popp is hoping to say goodbye to VfL Wolfsburg in the best way possible by winning one more women's German Cup with the club she called home for 14 years. "It would mean a lot to me to take the Cup once more back to Wolfsburg," Popp said ahead of the final against defending champions Bayern Munich on Thursday.

articles.title: NBA Playoffs: Thunder hold off Lakers in Game 4 for another sweep
articles.description: LOS ANGELES — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 35 points, Chet Holmgren made a tiebreaking dunk with 32.8 seconds to play, and the Oklahoma City Thunder swept the Los Angeles Lakers out of the second round of the NBA playoffs with a 115-110 victory in Game 4 on Monday night. Ajay Mitchell scored 10 of his 28 points in the frantic final period as the Thunder overcame the Lakers’ tenacious effort and improved to 8-0 in the playoffs with their toughest victory of the postseason. articles.title: Avs Make Changes, Beat Wild To Take 3-1 Series Lead
articles.description: By changing their lines, pairings and starting goaltender, the Colorado Avalanche now have changed the dynamics of their playoff series against the Minnesota Wild. The Avalanche, with goals from five different players, secured a 5-2 win Monday night that allowed them to swing the series from potentially being tied to holding a 3-1 lead and on the brink of their first Western Conference finals since 2022.
